Title: IWS - Verify occupancy detection status displays
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, in the Sensor Output section, locate the Occupancy field.
3. Observe the occupancy value with the space occupied by at least one person.
Preconditions: 1. The occupancy indicator shows a boolean occupied/unoccupied state.

Title: 2. The value updates in near real-time as the space becomes occupied or unoccupied.
IWS - Verify people count displays correctly
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, in the Sensor Output section, locate the People Count field.
3. Observe the count with a known number of people in the sensor field of view.
Preconditions: 1. The people count displays as an integer value.

Title: 2. The count reflects the approximate number of people detected by the thermal sensor.
3. The value updates in near real-time.
IWS - Verify people count with zero occupants
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
5. The sensor field of view is empty (no people present).
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, in the Sensor Output section, observe the People Count value.
2. The occupancy status shows unoccupied.
IWS - Verify luminosity sensor value and unit
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, in the Sensor Output section, locate the Luminosity field.
3. Observe the displayed value and unit.
Preconditions: 1. The luminosity value displays in Lux.

Title: 2. The value is within the range of 0–4,000 Lux.
3. The unit label "Lux" is shown.
IWS - Verify luminosity sensor boundary at 0 Lux
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
5. The sensor is in a completely dark environment (cover the light sensor).
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, in the Sensor Output section, observe the Luminosity reading.
Preconditions: 1. The luminosity value displays 0 Lux (or near 0).

Title: 2. No negative values are displayed.
IWS - Verify luminosity sensor boundary at max Lux
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
5. The sensor is exposed to bright light exceeding 4,000 Lux.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, in the Sensor Output section, observe the Luminosity reading.
Preconditions: 1. The luminosity value caps at 4,000 Lux or displays a maximum indicator.

Title: 2. No values above the documented maximum are shown.
IWS - Verify temperature sensor value in Celsius
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, in the Sensor Output section, locate the Temperature field.
3. Verify the unit is displayed as °C.
Preconditions: 1. The temperature value displays with the °C unit.

Title: 2. The value is within the operational range (+5°C to +35°C).
3. The reading is plausible for the current environment.
IWS - Verify temperature sensor value in Fahrenheit
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, in the Sensor Output section, locate the Temperature field.
3. Change the temperature unit to °F (if unit toggle is available in properties or Floating Window).
Preconditions: 1. The temperature value displays with the °F unit.

Title: 2. The value is the correct Fahrenheit equivalent of the Celsius reading.
3. The unit label updates to "°F".

4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, in the Sensor Output section, locate the Sound Level field.

IWS - Verify all sensor values update in near real-time
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, in the Sensor Output section, observe all sensor readings (Occupancy, People Count, Luminosity, temperature, humidity, TVOC, sound level).
3. Introduce a change in the environment (e.g., turn lights on/off, enter/exit the room, make noise).
4. Observe sensor values for updates.
Preconditions: 1. All affected sensor values update within a few seconds of the environmental change.

Title: 2. No sensor reading is frozen or displaying stale data.
IWS - Verify sensor values when device is disconnected
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device was previously commissioned and connected.
Preconditions: 1. Disconnect the IWS device from the network (unplug Ethernet cable).

Title: 2. Observe Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor" > Status tab > Sensor Output section readings.
3. Observe the component status on Center Workspace > Canvas.
Preconditions: 1. Sensor values stop updating or display a "no data" / fault indicator.

Title: 2. The component status on the canvas changes to indicate disconnected/fault state.
3. No crash or UI hang occurs.
IWS - Verify sensor values restore after reconnection
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device was previously commissioned, then disconnected.
Preconditions: 1. Reconnect the IWS device to the network (plug Ethernet cable back in).

Title: 2. Wait for the device to re-establish connection.
3. Observe Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor" > Status tab > Sensor Output section readings.
4. Observe the component status on the canvas.
Preconditions: 1. Sensor values resume updating with live data.

Title: 2. The component status returns to normal/connected state.
3. No manual intervention is required to restore data flow.
IWS - Verify dewpoint sensor value
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, in the Sensor Output section, locate the Dewpoint field.
3. Observe the displayed value.
Preconditions: 1. The dewpoint value displays as a numeric temperature value.

Title: 2. The value is plausible given the current temperature and humidity readings.
3. The unit matches the configured temperature unit (°C or °F).
IWS - Verify all sensor types are listed
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, review the Sensor Output section.
Preconditions: 1. All sensor types are present: Occupancy State, People Count, Luminosity, Temperature, Dewpoint, Humidity, TVOC, Sound Level.

Title: 2. Each sensor type has its correct unit label.
3. No sensor types are missing.
IWS - Verify sensor data with multiple IWS devices
2. Create a new design.
3. Add two IWS components to the canvas.
4. Both IWS devices are commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the first IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor" for IWS 1.

Title: 2. Double-click the second IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor" for IWS 2.
3. Compare sensor readings between the two devices.
Preconditions: 1. Each Floating Window displays independent sensor data for its respective device.

Title: 2. Sensor values are not cross-contaminated between devices.
3. Both windows can be open simultaneously.
IWS - Verify sensor data display in emulate mode
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. No physical IWS device is connected (emulate mode).
Preconditions: 1. Run the design in emulate mode.

Title: 2. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".
3. On the Status tab, observe the Sensor Output section.
Preconditions: 1. Sensor fields are present but may show default/zero values or "N/A".

Title: 2. The component does not display errors for missing hardware in emulate mode.
3. The UI remains fully navigable.
IWS - Verify sensor reading precision and formatting
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, in the Sensor Output section, review the decimal precision of each sensor value.
3. Note the formatting (decimal places, rounding).
Preconditions: 1. Luminosity displays as a whole number (Lux).

Title: 2. Temperature displays with appropriate precision (e.g., 1 decimal place).
3. Humidity displays with appropriate precision.
4. All values are formatted consistently and are human-readable.
